Published on 1:52pm GMT 11 April 2026

Pep Guardiola’s side are getting to full health at the right time and their only two defeats in their last 16 matches came home and away to Real Madrid in the Champions League. Recent wins over Arsenal and Liverpool read well and Chelsea are failing to fire, having lost four of their last five matches.

City have won four of their last five away Premier League games against Chelsea, the latest of which ended 2-0, and that may be the way to go. The hosts have failed to score in three of their last four games while City should be buoyed defensively by back-to-back clean sheets against Arsenal and Liverpool.

Erling Haaland to score anytime

Haaland has scored four times across his last five clashes with Chelsea, and he’s netted three goals across his last two visits to Stamford Bridge. Given how vulnerable the Blues are defensively, Haaland should build on last weekend’s hat-trick against Liverpool.

Cherki has eight Premier League assists for the season, and he faces a Chelsea team that was routinely opened up by Everton’s midfield in their last Premier League game. Add in that Moises Caicedo enters the game one booking shy of a suspension and Enzo Fernandez is out, and the Blues look soft in the middle of the park.

Gusto has picked up the majority of his cards this season while playing at right-back, and Jeremy Doku should trouble him this weekend. Doku has drawn 70 fouls across all competitions this term, at an average of over two per 90 minutes.

Nico O’Reilly to be carded

While Gusto, O’Reilly also receives the majority of his cards playing at full-back. Six of O’Reilly’s cards this term have come away from home, while Chelsea have drawn the third-most yellow cards in the league this term.

Having both booked their place in the FA Cup semi-final last weekend, this may not be the last time that Chelsea and Manchester City lock horns this season.

The Citizens brushed aside Liverpool 4-0 at the Etihad to set up a semi-final date with Southampton, while Chelsea ran riot in a 7-0 win over League One strugglers Port Vale to organise a last-four rumble with Leeds.

They could well meet in the FA Cup final at Wembley but there is plenty at stake for both in the Premier League first and City should be full of confidence.

The Citizens are hitting top stride at a crucial stage in the season, having defeated Arsenal in the EFL Cup final before inflicting that heavy beating on Liverpool.

Pep Guardiola’s side are getting to full health at the right time and their only two defeats in their last 16 matches came home and away to Real Madrid in the Champions League.

With eight games to go, City will be keen to put the pressure on leaders Arsenal but a nine-point gap at the start of this weekend means any slip up could be fatal.

In stark contrast, Chelsea had been in dismal form before putting seven past lower-league opposition in Port Vale and the pressure has been ramped up on manager Liam Rosenior.

The Blues signed off before the international break with a dismal 3-0 defeat at Everton and that came on the back of two losses to Paris St-Germain and 1-0 beating from Newcastle at Stamford Bridge.

Chelsea have won only one of their last six league games, which is why they started the weekend in sixth and have work to do to break into the Champions League places.

Rosenior still looks unsure on his favoured players in most positions and the decision to drop influential midfielder Enzo Fernandez for untimely comments shows some unrest in the camp.

The Citizens are unbeaten in 12 meetings with Chelsea, winning three of the last four, and that superiority should be upheld as the Blues have failed to win their last three Premier League home games.

City have won four of their last five away Premier League games against Chelsea, the latest of which ended 2-0, and that may be the way to go for correct-score punters.

The hosts have failed to score in three of their last four games while City should be buoyed defensively by back-to-back clean sheets against Arsenal and Liverpool.

Published on 2:00pm GMT 10 April 2026

Como have enjoyed an excellent season but they have tended to struggle against the teams above them, managing two losses and two draws in their four league games against Inter, AC Milan and Napoli. They were routed 4-0 in the reverse fixture and lack the quality of top-of-the-table Inter.

Inter should have enough to take three points at Como but shutting out their hosts could prove difficult. The Nerazzurri have managed only two clean sheets in their last five away games and Como, who put five past lowly Pisa in their last home outing, can grab a consolation.

A three-game winless run, one which began with a 1-0 defeat to rivals AC Milan, will have had Inter fans worrying their title charge was set to unravel but Cristian Chivu’s side alleviated those fears in emphatic fashion last weekend.

The Nerazzurri proved why they are sitting pretty at the Serie A summit by routing Roma 5-2 and they can overcome another tricky test when making the short trip to Como on Sunday.

Como themselves have enjoyed an excellent season and are firmly in the mix to finish in the top four. However, Inter have looked a cut above the other 19 Serie A teams all season and can prove their strength at the Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia.

Cesc Fabregas’s side were frustrated in a 0-0 draw with Udinese last time and that was hardly the ideal warmup for a visit from an Inter side who have won nine of their last 11 away games in Serie A.

Como are a brilliant team and play beautiful football, though that tends to serve them better against lesser sides. They were routed 4-0 by Inter in the reverse fixture and were also beaten 3-1 by Milan, another of the few teams above them, when hosting the Rossoneri in January.

That suggests that, while the Lariani are a strong outfit, Inter are simply too classy a side to oppose.

Published on 2:50pm GMT 10 April 2026

Despite enjoying a 13-game winning streak earlier in the season, Lyon find themselves on a run of nine matches without a victory and their luckless run may continue on Sunday. Visiting Lorient have lost just two of their last 17 matches and only two of their last ten on the road, so they should be backed to pick up at least a point.

Lorient are a huge price to win on Sunday, but they beat title-chasing Lens last month should not be dismissed easily. The visitors were 1-0 winners in the reverse fixture and with Lyon failing to find the net in three of their last four matches, a repeat scoreline is not out of the question.

A 1-0 defeat to Lorient in December proved to be a turning point for Lyon as Paulo Fonseca’s side strung together a stunning 13-match winning streak following that loss. However, they have lost their way since and will be hoping the reverse fixture with the Merlus can again be the springboard for improved form.

Lyon have followed their winning streak with a run of nine games without a victory and a measly expected-goals (xG) figure over 0.74 in last week’s 0-0 draw with Angers did little to suggest they will snap that sequence anytime soon.

Fonseca’s side definitely need to if they are to keep hold of their spot in the top six but Lorient have proved tough to beat for a long while now and could prove to be tricky customers.

Olivier Pantaloni’s men have moved into the top half after a run of just two losses in 17 Ligue 1 matches and their ability to up their game for the big sides was on full show last month.

Lorient came out 2-1 victors against title-chasing Lens and also earned a point at third-placed Lille in March, so they will be fearing nothing ahead of a trip to Parc Olympique Lyonnais.

Lyon are far too short given their current form and it would be no surprise to see Lorient take at least a point on Sunday.

Stamford Bridge sets the stage for the sizzling Sunday showdown, with Chelsea playing host to Manchester City on Matchday 32 in the Premier League. The Blues are in sixth place, while the Citizens are second as we enter the final stretch of the season. Check out our match prediction below!

Chelsea’s hopes of featuring in Europe’s elite club competition next season received a major boost last week following confirmation that the EPL will be allowed five teams in the 2026-27 Champions League. However, the West Londoners will need to turn things around here, having gone into the international break after back-to-back league defeats.

Meanwhile, the EPL title race is now once again wide-open after Arsenal suffered a costly defeat at home to Bournemouth on Saturday. Pep Guardiola’s side still trail the Gunners by nine points, but they have two games in hand and are set to face Mikel Arteta’s men at home next weekend at the Etihad in a potential league decider. Let’s get into the key stats!

Fair to say, March was a tough month for Chelsea. After sneaking past Wrexham in the FA Cup quarter-final, they suffered four consecutive defeats, a period during which they were dumped out of the UCL.

However, the international break may have been just what the doctor ordered for the Blues, as they returned to action with an emphatic 7-0 victory over Port Vale to secure their spot in the FA Cup semis last week.

That victory followed four games without victory at Stamford Bridge for this week’s hosts (D2 L2). They have earned only 23 points in 15 league home games this season, which is the 12th-best record in the division.

Chelsea are winless in their last five EPL home games against Man City!

Meanwhile, Manchester City went into the international break off the back of winning the Carabao Cup against Arsenal and returned with a 4-0 hammering of Liverpool in the FA Cup last week.

However, the Citizens will now be hoping to get back to winning ways in the top flight following back-to-back draws against West Ham United and Nottingham Forest on MD30 and 31, respectively.

The visitors are unbeaten in their last four away league games ahead of this one (W2 D2). They have taken 25 points from their 15 league games on the road this term - the joint-second-highest tally in the top flight.

The biggest talking point around Chelsea in recent weeks has been the ban handed to Enzo Fernandez. Despite attempting to clear the air with management, Liam Rosenior confirmed that the former Benfica man will not be allowed to return for this match.

Cole Palmer is therefore likely to move into the number 10 role this weekend behind Joao Pedro. The Brazilian has 13 goal contributions in his last 10 league games, including six in his last five EPL home matches.

During his pre-match press conference, Rosenior confirmed that Reece James, Trevoh Chalobah and Levi Colwill are back in training but are still a few weeks away from returning to action. Jamie Gittens and Filip Joregensen are still sidelined through injury.

Erling Haaland has three goal contributions in his last three league games against Chelsea!

The Norwegian’s output has dipped significantly since the start of the year, but he netted a hat-trick in the victory over Liverpool in the FA Cup last week, and will be determined to follow up in this match.

Antoine Semenyo moved to 24 goal contributions for the season by setting up Haaland for his second against the Reds, and the Ghanaian will almost certainly operate as part of the midfield attacking trio this week, along with Jeremy Doku and Rayan Cherki.

The visitors will be missing three players for this trip to the capital - all defensive - in John Stones, Rube Dias and Josko Gvardiol.

Chelsea endured a difficult run of results leading up to the March international break, but they returned to action with a massive victory over Port Vale, which would have given them a much-needed boost of confidence.

Seven of Chelsea’s last eight games in all competitions have ended with over 2.5 goals, while five of Man City’s last seven have seen three or more goals strike the back of the net.

Manchester City are unbeaten in their last eight league games, while Chelsea have lost three of their last four in the top flight. Additionally, City are unbeaten in their last five league visits to Stamford Bridge.

Como’s push for a top-four finish in Serie A faces a test of their credentials against leaders Inter Milan on matchday 32. This match on Sunday at Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia between the fourth-placed ‘Lariani’ and the first-placed ‘Nerazzurri’ comes with our prediction of less than 2.5 goals.

Ahead of this round of fixtures, Como have 58 points (W16, D10, L5) and are only one point above sixth-placed Roma and fifth-placed Juventus. Inter are at the top of the standings with 72 points (W23, D3, L5) and are seven points above Napoli, who are their nearest challengers.

The top-four race in Serie A is proving to be a tight battle between Como, Juventus, and Roma. Points could be precious in this context, and the hosts can’t afford to lose here. These sides are set to meet in the second leg of the Coppa Italia semi-finals later this month, where the first leg finished 0-0. Will Como’s season-defining run start with a win over Inter Milan? Read more about our analysis, team news, and match predictions.

Como drew 0-0 away at Udinese last weekend to extend their unbeaten run to eight competitive matches (W5, D3). That included a 0-0 draw against Inter in the first leg of the Coppa Italia. Como’s form in the league is even more impressive, winning five of their last seven league games (D2).

That run included three consecutive wins at home, where Como aggregated ten goals. That puts them in line to win four consecutive home top-flight games for the first time since 1980.

Inter won their last ten Serie A matches against Como.

Inter were 5-2 winners over Roma last weekend, ending their four-game wait for a win in all competitions (D3, L1). The Nerazzurri are still front runners in the title race, and with seven rounds remaining, including this, their seven-point lead over Napoli could prove enough.

Nine wins from their last 11 away Serie A games (D1, L1) is a strong indicator of Inter’s form on the road. That’s only half the story, as the two failures to win came in their last two trips.

Jacobo Ramon and Jesus Rodriguez were absentees for Como in their draw against Udinese. The duo is likely to be available here, although their participation from the start is doubtful. Also missing out for the hosts is Jayden Addai.

The hosts have usually lined up in a 4-2-3-1 system, with the red-hot Anastasios Douvikas starting as the lone striker. He has 11 league goals to his name this season and remains their best bet to find the back of the net.

Nico Paz has ten goals and six assists in Serie A this season.

Inter were dealt a serious blow when captain Lautaro Martinez was ruled out for three weeks. He joins Yann Bisseck on the sidelines for the visitors, although the return of Carlos Augusto from suspension gives them a boost.

The visitors are likely to stick to their 3-5-2 system, with Pio Esposito joining Marcus Thuram as the two strikers upfront. Lautaro’s absence takes out their best striker this season, with the Argentine scoring 16 league goals. Thuram has eight Serie A goals to his name, while Hakan Calhanoglu chipped in with nine strikes.

This match has important implications for Como, as they are tangled in a tight race to finish fourth. With Juventus and Roma starting this round only one point behind, a loss here could see Como tumble as low as sixth. Inter started the round with a seven-point lead at the top and could afford to concentrate on the second leg of the Coppa Italia semi-finals, also against Como, where the first leg finished 0-0.

Como don’t boast a great record against Inter Milan, but this could be their chance to change that. They have been defensively great this season, and repeating that feat could at least give them a point.

Lyon will be looking to arrest their extending winless run when they welcome Lorient for yet another tough Ligue 1 outing. Ahead of this game, Lyon are sixth three places above Lorient, and the two sides are 10 points apart. Let’s have a look at the match prediction.

A side once on 13 straight wins, Lyon have been a pale shadow of their former selves. They’ve failed to win any of their last six league matches, and form has abandoned them at the most inopportune period.

Gunning for Champions League football, once tucked in third place, Lyon have now been pushed to four points adrift after they could only manage a 0-0 draw with Angers.

Lorient aren’t the easiest of opponents, and Lyon will know better, having lost to them 1-0 in the reverse match. Across the last five meetings between these two, Lorient have only suffered one defeat, though they won twice in the said period.

They are also coming off a draw, having played to a 1-1 outcome at home against Paris FC. Les Merlus will be confident of upsetting Lyon, having won three more points than Les Gones across the last five rounds.

Their away form is what could give them away, with the visitors failing to win in the last four, losing twice. Each of their last three away games at Lyon ended in draws.

Competitively, Lyon have now failed to win any of their last nine matches since their impressive winning run was ended. They’ve only gone winless in 10 games three times in their history and would love to avoid equaling that record.

This has perhaps been the most electric matchup between two teams in the NBA all season. The Spurs took the first game against Denver in November, winning 139-136 on the road. Fast forward to March, and the Nuggets got their revenge with a brilliant 136-131 win in this stadium. Then just eight days ago, Denver ran it back with a 136-134 OT win on these grounds.

With both teams qualified for the playoffs as the 2nd and 3rd seeds, the chances of meeting in the later stages are quite high. San Antonio has been absolutely dominant, even against the reigning champions, OKC. But if they can’t solve their Denver problems, then they'd better hope someone does before they potentially meet.

Manchester United have won all five of their home games under Michael Carrick’s watch, and they should prove too strong for a Leeds side that have picked up the third fewest away points this season.

The visitors have seen both teams score in seven of their last eight away games, while United have kept only three clean sheets in 10 games under Carrick, including concededing in three of their last four home wins.

Fernandes has scored five of his eight Premier League goals this season at home, while he’s had a hand in six goals across his last four appearances. The Portuguese has thrived against weaker teams, scoring against Wolves, Burnley, and Tottenham this term.

Manchester United face their first fixture in 25 days on Monday night, having been given a watching brief since the opening match of the last round of league action. With no European or FA Cup fixtures, the Red Devils have had plenty of time to prepare for the visit of Leeds.

The visitors have played twice since United’s last game, including a penalty shoot-out win at West Ham in last weekend’s FA Cup quarter-finals. Daniel Farke’s side are without a league win in over two months, which has left them firmly in the relegation dogfight.

No side has picked up more Premier League points in 2026 than United, but the Red Devils have won only one of their last three games, dating back to the 4th of March. Michael Carrick’s side should have plenty in the tank for the run-in, but they need to shake off any rustiness ahead of a key game.

Home form has been key to Leeds’ success this term, which has compensated for their struggles on the road. A 3-1 victory at bottom side Wolves in September is their only league win away from Elland Road this season.

While they’ve drawn their last four away trips, only Burnley and Wolves have picked up fewer points on their travels. The visitors have shipped eight goals across their defeats at Arsenal and Man City, while they’ve conceded close to two goals per game overall.

United have won all five of their home games under Michael Carrick, and they’ve scored at least twice in each of those victories. His approach of putting together a fluid trio of attackers ahead of Bruno Fernandes has been incredibly effective, while Benjamin Sesko has been a real scoring threat late on from the bench.

Fernandes has scored five of his eight Premier League goals at Old Trafford this season, and he netted from the penalty spot against Bournemouth last time out. He’s had a hand in six goals across his last four games under Carrick, while he’s found the net in clashes against strugglers like Burnley, Wolves, and Tottenham.

Fernandes should be backed to continue his fine form in front of goal, and he can inspire United to a sixth straight win at Old Trafford. With seven of Leeds’ last eight away trips across all competitions seeing both teams score, the hosts are backed for a 2-1 win after conceding in three of their last four home fixtures.

The Roses rivalry will reignite at Old Trafford on Monday night, when Manchester United play host to Leeds United in the final fixture of Matchday 32 in the Premier League. The Red Devils are third in the league standings, while the Whites are in 15th place after 31 games. Check out our match prediction below!

The 2025-26 EPL season has been a tale of two halves for Man United. After a poor start to the campaign, the Red Devils have turned things around incredibly, losing only once under Michael Carrick, while having earned the second-most points (29) in the top flight since Christmas, only behind Arsenal’s tally of 31.

While United are on their way to UCL qualification, the remainder of the season for Leeds is all about survival, as they start MD32 just three points above the drop. The Peacocks held this opponent to a 1-1 draw in the reverse fixture back in January, but can they take anything from this clash on enemy territory? Let’s get into the key stats!

Manchester United saw their lead above fourth-placed Aston Villa trimmed to just a single point after being held to a 2-2 draw by Bournemouth directly before the March international break.

The Red Devils have now lost only one of their last 14 EPL matches since December 21 and have scored two or more goals in seven of their last 10 games. That sole defeat came against Newcastle at the start of last month.

The hosts have been excellent at home of late, currently riding a five-match win streak and unbeaten in their last nine league games. They have also scored two or more goals in each home game under Carrick this season.

Man United have not dropped a point at home in the EPL under Michael Carrick!

While it has been 24 days since the Red Devils last played, Leeds United were in action last weekend, when they survived a massive scare to beat West Ham on penalties in the FA Cup quarter-finals.

However, the Whites entered the international break following consecutive goalless draws against Brentford and Palace and are now winless in their last six league matches since early February (D4 L2).

The visitors have drawn each of their last four away league games and have not won on the road in 12 matches. They have earned just 10 points in 15 away games this season, which is the third-worst record in the top flight.

Bruno Fernandes has been nothing short of excellent for Man United this season. The team’s skipper has created 101 chances in the league this season and netted his eighth goal of the campaign against the Cherries. He now has 24 goal contributions in 28 EPL games.

Carrick will be forced into a reshuffle at the back for this match after Harry Maguire was shown red at the Vitality last time out. Lisandro Martinez returned to training during the international break and could replace Maguire in this one.

The hosts have two confirmed injury absentees in Matthijs De Ligt and Patrick Dorgu. Benjamin Sesko and Bryan Mbeumo are listed as minor doubts but have not been ruled out for this match.

Fernandes has eight goal contributions in six league matches against Leeds United!

Daniel Farke opted to start Lukas Nmecha and Noah Okafor as an attacking duo against the Hammers, but Dominic Calvert-Lewin came off the bench to score the team’s second goal in that match and should get the nod to start this one.

However, the FA Cup triumph came at a cost to the Peacocks, with both Anton Stach and Joe Rodon sustaining ankle injuries, which will see them watch this match from the sidelines.

Jaka Bijol is listed as a doubt owing to a knock, while Gabriel Gudmundson is still recovering from a strained thigh muscle and is unlikely to feature in this one.

The reverse fixture between these sides marked the beginning of the end for Ruben Amorim, but since then, Manchester United have not lost a single league game at home and have been one of the hottest teams in the Prem.

Bruno Fernandes has 24 goal contributions in 28 EPL games and eight goal contributions in six league matches against Leeds United.

Manchester United have scored 30 goals in 15 home league games this season, and have netted two or more in each of their last five at Old Trafford under Michael Carrick.

Published on 6:18pm GMT 10 March 2025

PSG dominated much of the first leg and against a Liverpool side showing signs of fatigue over recent games, the visitors can at least avoid defeat in this return match, especially as last week’s loss represents their only defeat across their last 24 games.

PSG have scored two or more goals in nine of their last 10 away fixtures, but a lack of clean-sheets coupled with Liverpool’s own scoring ability means the Reds can also get on the scoresheet. That brings a 2-1 away win into play after 90 minutes, which would mean extra time and perhaps penalties to decide the tie.

One of Europe’s in-form players, Ousmane Dembele has scored 20 goals for PSG since the turn of the year, including two from the bench against Rennes on Saturday. Having had eight shots without scoring during the first leg, the France international will be desperate to make his mark at Anfield.

Liverpool will take a slender 1-0 lead into the second leg of their Champions League last 16 tie with PSG at Anfield after Harvey Elliott’s late goal handed them a rather fortuitous victory in Paris last midweek.

Arne Slot’s side had been on the backfoot for much of the game at the Parc des Princes and had goalkeeper Alisson to thank for still being in the contest before substitute Elliott slotted home late on, but there were certainly signs of fatigue from the Premier League leaders.

That was also true during Saturday’s 3-1 come-from-behind success over struggling Southampton, a result that boosted the Reds’ hopes of Premier League glory, but underlined that the push for both domestic and European honours may be taking its toll.

PSG will feel that if they can match their performance levels from the first leg then they will be able to turn the tie around and they will come into the game off the back of a 4-1 success over Rennes in Ligue 1.

Unlike Liverpool boss Slot, who went with a near full strength starting line up against Southampton, PSG head coach Luis Enrique opted to rest several of his key players, although forward Ousmane Dembele did come off the bench to net twice late on.

The Parisians have won 11 of their last 12 matches across all competitions – the exception being last week’s first leg – which is also their only defeat in 24 games, a run that dates back to November.

To say PSG forward Dembele is in form would be something of an understatement, with the France international having netted 20 goals since the turn of the year, including his brace against Rennes.

Dembele had eight shots during last week’s first leg and he could have had a couple of goals at least on another day, something he will be keen to put right at Anfield.

Against a Liverpool side showing signs of fatigue and whom they dominated for much of the first leg, PSG are capable of edging things on Merseyside and that could perhaps result in the match going to extra time.

PSG have scored two or more goals in nine of their last 10 away fixtures, but their only clean-sheet in their last five games, home or away, was against fourth tier Stade Briochin in the Coupe de France.

Liverpool have scored in 19 consecutive home games and that trend can certainly continue on Tuesday, but PSG can avoid defeat on the night at the very least.

Published on 3:38pm GMT 10 April 2026

Fulham have only lost one of the last five meetings between these two teams and they could at least avoid defeat against a Liverpool side whose confidence will be rock bottom after losing five of their last eight games. They may have one eye on the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final against PSG and could fail to win again.

Meetings between these two teams have had a tendency to be high-scoring affairs, with five of the last six going over 2.5 goals, and a repeat of that could be on the cards given Liverpool’s poor defensive record. Side with a 2-2 draw in this one.

With Alexander Isak still working his way back from injury, Hugo Ekitike is likely to lead the line again for Liverpool and he could get on the scoresheet for what would be the 18th time in all competitions this season.

Liverpool’s disastrous campaign continued with a 2-0 humbling away to Paris Saint-Germain on Wednesday in the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final.

In reality, the Reds were lucky to keep PSG to a two-goal lead, losing the shot count 18-3 and seeing a couple of penalty decisions go their way.

Arne Slot set up in a back five in that game to avoid a hammering and it may be hard for his side to switch back to controlling the game as they host Fulham in the Premier League on Saturday evening.

The Cottagers are just five points behind the home side after a solid season, only the top five have won more games than Marco Silva’s men and they are in the mix for a European place.

They denied Liverpool in spectacular fashion in the reverse fixture, with a 97th-minute Harrison Reed wonderstrike cancelling out what had looked like the winner from Cody Gakpo in the 94th minute.

That continued a solid record for Fulham against these opponents, they have only lost one of the last five head-to-heads, and they could be facing them at the perfect time with the Reds just days away from the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final.

Slot’s men look devoid of confidence having lost five of their last eight games and with the side out of the League Cup, FA Cup and adrift of Arsenal in their Premier League title defence, Europe is now their only slim chance of winning some silverware this season.

Things could go from bad to worse with Fulham fancied to at least avoid defeat at Anfield. The visitors will have freshened up with the three weeks off and have won two and drawn one of their last three away games.

They could be the latest team to take advantage of a drastically below par Liverpool side who may be tempted to make a change in the dugout sooner rather than later as the pressure mounts on manager Arne Slot.

Games between these two teams have a habit of being entertaining, with both teams scoring in the last seven meetings between the pair, and the same is fancied this time around.

Two of the last three head-to-heads have finished 2-2 and a repeat of that looks a runner considering Liverpool’s defensive frailties.

At the other end of the pitch, Hugo Ekitike looks a tempting price to get on the scoresheet with Alexander Isak still being eased back in after injury. Ekitike has 17 goals in all competitions and could add to that tally.

Published on 9:50pm GMT 27 November 2025

Bayern v St Pauli has strong potential for goals. Bayern have scored in 23 straight games, lead the league with ten wins from 11, and have seen over 3.5 goals land in seven of those matches. St Pauli’s defensive issues include 21 goals conceded and 17 games without a clean sheet.

Heavy scoring trends support a 3-1 outcome, with Bayern winning seven of their last nine league games by at least two goals. St Pauli have lost eight straight and concede 1.91 goals per game, while Bayern’s strong home form — 18 wins in their last 20 — underpins the margin.

Bayern Munich have scored in 23 consecutive games and Saturday’s clash with St Pauli at the Allianz Arena has the potential to be a goalfest. Back over 3.5 goals.

Bayern Munich are top of the league, having won ten of their 11 games this season. In contrast, St Pauli sit 16th in the table with two wins and eight defeats.

Bayern Munich have won their last eight Bundesliga home games and St Pauli are on a run of poor form, with eight defeats in succession.

No team has had more Bundesliga games with over 3.5 goals than Bayern Munich this season, and it has been a winning bet in seven of their 11 games.

The visitors have been poor defensively this season, with St Pauli failing to keep a clean sheet in 17 of their last 19 Bundesliga games. They have conceded 21 goals in 11 games – an average of 1.91 goals per game.

Bayern Munich have won seven of their last nine Bundesliga matches by at least a two-goal margin and have enjoyed success in 18 of their last 20 home games. A 3-1 win for Bayern is an appealing correct-score bet.

Published on 1:55pm GMT 9 April 2026

Both teams have scored in four of Barcelona’s last six matches and also in nine of Espanyol’s last 10 games. The Parakeets created three big chances and an xG of 1.6 in their 2-0 home loss to Barca in January, so should carve out some chances if they follow a similar blueprint while Barca have failed to score only three times this term.

Barcelona have scored multiple goals in eight of their last 10 home games while Espanyol have conceded more than once in five of their last six away trips. They have tightened up a little of late, conceding no more than two in any of their last five and could go down 2-1 with their heads held high on Saturday.

The Barcelona derby arrives at a fascinating moment in the Catalan giants’ campaign – slap bang in between two massive Champions League quarter-final ties against Atletico Madrid.

Hansi Flick’s side are still absorbing the impact of their 2-0 loss in the first leg at Spotify Camp Nou, which has left their European hopes hanging by a thread. Domestically, they remain in control — seven points clear at the top of La Liga and in excellent league form, having beaten Atleti and Villarreal during a six‑match winning streak.

Barca average nearly three goals per game in the league and dominate possession with their trademark control. Even with injuries to Frenkie De Jong, Andreas Christensen and Raphinha,  plus fresh doubts about Pedri’s fitness, they should field a side with enough quality to win well.

Espanyol, meanwhile, have slipped badly since the turn of the year. Winless in 2026, with eight defeats in 13 games, their early‑season push for Europe has evaporated. Yet they still create chances, and their direct style — plus Barcelona’s occasional defensive lapses — suggests they can contribute to an open derby.

Flick’s side are so short in the betting that it is better to look elsewhere for the value, which is probably around both teams to score. Barcelona have only failed to score three times all season, while Espanyol’s need for a statement performance should push them forward.

The Parakeets went close on a few occasions in their 2-0 home defeat to Barca in January and have scored and conceded in nine of their last 10 games.

Published on 1:00pm GMT 9 April 2026

Both teams have scored in eight of Atalanta’s last 10 games and also in six of Juventus’ last 10 games. And with so much riding on the result, neither team is likely to sit back.

Three of the last four Serie A meetings of these clubs were drawn, two finishing 1-1, which could be the way of things again given each side is coming in with solid unbeaten form.

With the Serie A season entering its decisive stretch, Atalanta and Juventus continue their challenge for European places at New Balance Arena in Bergamo. Both clubs endured inconsistent starts to the season, but have played themselves into contention with a few games to go.

Atalanta’s 3-0 win over Lecce last weekend secured seven points from their last three league games, including a 1-1 draw at leaders Inter. Gianluca Scamacca’s ongoing adductor issue remains a major concern so Nikola Krstovic is expected to deputise again, while Isak Hien is also a doubt.

Rafaelle Palladino’s side know that victory would pull them within a point of fifth-placed Juve and keep their Europa League hopes firmly alive.

The Old Lady, meanwhile, travel north with their Champions League ambitions on the line. A point behind Como, they cannot afford a slip, but injuries are taking a toll— Mattia Perin is struggling with a calf problem and Dusan Vlahovic’s return has been delayed. Kenan Yildiz and Jonathan David should again lead the attack after the 2-0 win over Genoa made it four wins in an unbeaten run of six.

Both sides arrive in decent spirits after weekend victories, and with so much riding on the result, neither is likely to sit back. Atalanta’s attacking approach at home and Juve’s need for points should create an open contest.

The overwhelming trend in Atalanta’s post-Christmas games is for goals at both ends and with six of Juve’s last eight also landing a BTTS bet, that is the top play. Take a 1-1 correct score too given thee of the last four direct meetings have been drawn, two by that exact score.

Published on 3:35pm GMT 9 April 2026

Both teams have scored with over 2.5 goals in Sevilla’s last two games against top-five clubs and also in five of Atletico Madrid’s last seven matches. Sevilla should see this as a chance to take points against a weakened Atleti side but their defensive issues could also betray them.

Sevilla have conceded 11 goals in their last five games and look unlikely to avoid shipping a couple again. Back a 2-2 draw as they try to raise themselves out of relegatuion danger with an attacking response.

Alexander Sorloth scored off the bench on Wednesday and has 10 La Liga goals this term. He looks set to start in a much-changed line-up ahead of the Champions League QF.

Sevilla enter Saturday’s clash with Atletico Madrid in full crisis mode after a third straight defeat and with Luis Garcia Plaza already under an ultimatum.

The new coach’s debut could hardly have gone worse — a damaging defeat away to bottom‑placed Oviedo — and Sevilla’s slide since their derby draw with Betis has been alarming. One point from four matches, including a heavy beating by Barcelona, has dragged them dangerously close to the relegation zone.

The Sanchez Pizjuan outfit score just 1.2 goals per game and concede nearly two, and their defensive structure continues to crumble at key moments. With Jose Carmona and Tanguy Nianzou suspended and several injuries piling up, Plaza must improvise again.

Two straight league defeats had halted Atletico’s momentum until they took control of their Champions League quarter-final against Barca in midweek.

But with one foot already in the semi‑finals of that competition and a comfortable cushion in the top‑four race, Diego Simeone is expected to ring the changes. Alexander Sorloth, who came off the bedcn to score the second at Camp Nou on Wednesday, looks set to start and can add to his 10-goal la Liga tally against a weak Sevilla backline.

Los Colchoneros are probably best swerved at short odds in the result market, but their attacking nature is unlikely to change much with the personnel, so back both teams to score with at least three goals in a game Sevilla dare not lose.

Published on 7:49am GMT 10 April 2026

Both teams have scored in six of Wrexham’s last seven matches. High-scoring games have been the norm for them this season. Birmingham have conceded in four of their last five home games, but can use home advantage to get on the scoresheet against a side that shipped five at home in midweek. Both teams have scored in the last six meetings of the clubs.

Wrexham have hit 63 goals this season (1.54 per game) and may just be able to nick this 2-1 with the Blues having not scored muiltiple goals since Norwich eight games ago.
Both sides concede heavily — Birmingham 52, Wrexham 58, so this is unlikely to stay 0-0 for long.

Red Dragons seek response in ‘Hollywood derby’

Wrexham arrive at St Andrew’s on Sunday needing a response after a bruising 5-1 midweek defeat to Southampton knocked them out of the Championship play‑off places. Phil Parkinson has been quick to insist that the setback cannot define their season, and with five games left, the Reds remain firmly in the top-six hunt.

There is less at stake for Birmingham in what they have been calling the ‘Hollywood derby’ after Chris Davies’ men faded from promotion contention earlier this spring. The Blues sit 16th in the table after a run of three consecutive defeats,  but they will be up for the clash against their League One promotion rivals from last season.

Wrexham’s attacking output has rarely been the issue. With 63 goals scored and Josh Windass in excellent form — three goals in his last two games — they carry enough threat to trouble any side. Parkinson wants a return to the “hallmark performances” that have driven the club’s rise, and a reaction is expected.

Birmingham, meanwhile, have continued to score at a decent rate, averaging 1.2 goals per game and finding the net in all three recent meetings with the Red Dragons. Their defensive record, like Wrexham’s, remains leaky, and that combination points strongly toward an open contest with goals at both ends.

With both teams needing a lift and both defences vulnerable, this has all the ingredients for a lively encounter. Back both teams to score as the main bet because Wrexham have conceded six goals in their last three away games while Birmingham were beaten 3-1 at home by Middlesbrough in March the last time they entertained a promotion-chasing side.

But the Blues have not scored more than once since a 2-1 win at Norwich in February and they will probably need to double up to take maximum points.

Published on 2:18pm GMT 9 April 2026

Sunderland have lost their last three home matches but a lot of that was down to an injury crisis which has since eased. The Black Cats were worthy winners in the Tyne-Wear derby before the international break and they are a much more cohesive side than Tottenham, who have gone 13 league games without a victory. Enigmatic manager Roberto De Zerbi has been tasked with turning things around but he took six games to get his first win at Brighton and is not sure to make an immediately positive impact.

Picking up wins may have proved tough during the short-lived Igor Tudor era but scoring goals wasn’t an issue as Tottenham failed to find the net in only one of their seven games under the Croatian. Roberto De Zerbi’s attacking tactics should see them find some joy on Wearside but given they have conceded at least two goals in nine of their last ten games, it is hard to see them keeping Sunderland quiet at the other end.

Brian Brobbey salvaged a point for Sunderland in January’s reverse fixture at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and he can haunt Spurs again. The Dutch striker was the hero in the Black Cats’ derby win over Sunderland before the international break and will be full of confidence.

Igor Tudor’s miserable reign as Tottenham manager was brought to an end over the international break and given Spurs start the weekend just one point above the Premier League relegation zone, they cannot afford for Roberto De Zerbi to follow in his predecessor’s footsteps.

The combined failures of Thomas Frank and Tudor have left De Zerbi with a mammoth job to do but the good news is that while there are just seven games left, the bar is low – Tudor failed to win any of his five Premier League games in the Spurs dugout.

A first league win of 2026 is the aim in the Italian’s first game at Sunderland on Sunday but the Lilywhites’ league winless run now stands at 13 games and a team short of confidence are unlikely to earn a positive result on Wearside.

De Zerbi isn’t exactly known for making a fast start, with it taking him six games to earn a win at Brighton during his last stint in England, and Sunderland are much more trustworthy in this current spot.

A 2-1 Tyne-Wear derby win at Newcastle meant the Black Cats signed off for the international break in the perfect fashion and Regis Le Bris’ men can build on that on Sunday.

Doubters will point towards their three-game losing streak at the Stadium of Light but a lot of that can be attributed to an injury crisis which has since eased, with excellent goalkeeper Robin Roefs and star defender Daniel Ballard set to return this weekend.

Spurs fans need De Zerbi’s attacking ideas to work instantly and they could, but that does not solve the issues at the other end. The Lilywhites conceded 20 goals in seven games under Tudor and it is a big ask for them to keep Sunderland quiet.

Tottenham may have enough to score on Wearside but three points should evade them again.
